notes: Collection of bias benchmarks for evaluating language model fairness: BBQ (Bias Benchmark for QA), WinoBias, StereoSet, CrowS-Pairs.

data.description: Tests social biases in question answering across 9 protected categories using ambiguous and disambiguated scenarios.
data.notes: 58,492 examples across 9 bias categories: age, disability, gender, nationality, physical appearance, race/ethnicity, religion, socioeconomic status, sexual orientation. Tests both accuracy and bias. Google research collaboration. Published 2022.

versions_available.note: First edition. 58,492 examples across 9 bias categories.

data.note: No per-category URL. Categories are subsets within the BBQ dataset. Nine categories: age, disability, gender, nationality, physical-appearance, race-ethnicity, religion, socioeconomic-status, sexual-orientation.

examples.note: Sexism and gender role stereotypes evaluation

data.description: Tests gender bias in coreference resolution systems using Winograd-schema inspired sentence pairs with 40 occupations.
data.notes: Winograd-schema inspired benchmark. Tests coreference systems for occupation-gender bias using pro-stereotype and anti-stereotype sentence pairs. 40 occupations with gender statistics. Widely used baseline for gender bias evaluation.

data.description: Measures stereotypical bias across four domains (gender, race, religion, profession) using intrasentence and intersentence tests.
data.notes: 17,000 test instances. Measures stereotype preference while balancing language quality. Uses Language Model Score (lms), Stereotype Score (ss), and ICAT (Idealized CAT) metrics. Targets specific demographic groups.

data.description: Evaluates stereotypes through minimal pair comparisons across 9 bias types. 1,508 crowdsourced sentence pairs.
data.notes: 1,508 sentence pairs comparing stereotyping vs anti-stereotyping sentences. Covers 9 bias types: race/color, gender, sexual orientation, religion, age, nationality, disability, physical appearance, socioeconomic status. 50% stereotype score indicates unbiased model. Covers intersectional biases.
